text:
CPU pressure is
a Linux kernel metric (PSI - Pressure Stall Information). Let me check how mongotune
reads
it.
• Read(crates/mongotune-core/src/gpu.rs)
L Read 250 lines
CPU pressure comes from Linux's PSI (Pressure Stall Information) subsystem, read
from /proc/pressure/cpu.
The Concept
Traditional CPU metrics (user%, system, idle%) tell you how busy the CPU was. PSI tells you something different: how much work is waiting
ecause there's not enough CPU.
It measures the percentage of time that tasks were
stalled-runnable but couldn't run because
all CPUs were busy.
The File
$ cat /proc/pressure/cpu
some
avg10=2.50 avg60=1.20 avg300=0.80 total=123456789
- some = at least one task was stalled
- avg10/60/300 = rolling averages over 10s/60s/5min (percentage)
- total = cumulative microseconds of stall time since boot
How Mongotune Uses It
Mongotune reads the total field and computes the rate between ticks (line 114-123):
rate = (current_total - previous_total) / elapsed
_microseconds
This gives a value between 0.0 and 1.0:
- 0.0 = no tasks waiting for CPU
- 1.0 = tasks were waiting for CPU 100% of the time
The default threshold is 0.99-meaning CPU pressure is only flagged when tasks were stalled nearly all the time between ticks.
Why Use Both Pressure AND Utilization?
High CPU utilization alone doesn't mean there's
a problem-the system might be happily processing work. High pressure
• Mongotune requires both to avoid
false positives: the CPUs are busy AND work is backing up.
work is piling up
